requirements.title: Agent library package and SDK integration
requirements.summary: Implement the coding agent as a Python library layered on the existing Unified LLM SDK, using low-level Client.complete and Client.stream rather than the SDK high-level generate loop.
acceptance_criteria: A Python package module for the agent loop is importable from the existing src layout without adding a required CLI entrypoint.
The public agent API exposes sessions, configuration, provider profiles, execution environments, events, and built-in tool registration points.
The implementation imports and uses Unified LLM SDK Client, Request, Response, Message, ContentPart, Role, Tool, ToolCall, ToolResult, StreamEvent, Usage, and FinishReason types where applicable.
Agent model turns are executed through low-level Client.complete and Client.stream calls and do not call the SDK high-level generate tool loop.
Validation remains uv-compatible with pytest and ruff, and library diagnostics use standard library logging instead of direct printing.
requirements.notes: Applies state constraints: Python implementation, Python package layout with pyproject.toml, uv/pytest validation, ruff linting, logging, and handled or appropriately logged exceptions.

requirements.title: Session, configuration, lifecycle, and turn records
requirements.summary: Define the core session state model, configurable limits/defaults, lifecycle states, and typed conversation turn records used by the agent loop.
acceptance_criteria: Session instances carry a UUID, ProviderProfile, ExecutionEnvironment, ordered history, event emitter, SessionConfig, SessionState, Unified LLM Client, steering queue, follow-up queue, and active subagent map.

SessionConfig implements defaults for unlimited max_turns and max_tool_rounds_per_input, 10000 ms default command timeout, 600000 ms max command timeout, nullable reasoning_effort, tool output and line limits, loop detection enabled, loop detection window 10, and max_subagent_depth 1.

SessionState represents IDLE, PROCESSING, AWAITING_INPUT, and CLOSED with transitions matching submit, natural completion, turn limit, unrecoverable error, explicit close, abort, and user answer flows.
UserTurn, AssistantTurn, ToolResultsTurn, SystemTurn, and SteeringTurn preserve the required content, tool calls, reasoning, usage, response ID, result list, and timestamp fields.
Session construction, lifecycle updates, and turn appends are observable through public APIs and tests without requiring live provider calls.
requirements.notes: Use Python dataclasses, enums, or protocols consistent with the existing SDK style.

requirements.title: Typed session event stream
requirements.summary: Emit every agent action through a typed async event stream suitable for host applications, UIs, logs, and tests.
acceptance_criteria: SessionEvent includes kind, timestamp, session_id, and a data mapping.

EventKind includes SESSION_START, SESSION_END, USER_INPUT, PROCESSING_END, ASSISTANT_TEXT_START, ASSISTANT_TEXT_DELTA, ASSISTANT_TEXT_END, TOOL_CALL_START, TOOL_CALL_OUTPUT_DELTA, TOOL_CALL_END, STEERING_INJECTED, TURN_LIMIT, LOOP_DETECTION, WARNING, and ERROR.
Events are delivered through an async iterator or equivalent Python async interface without requiring polling private state.
SESSION_START and SESSION_END bracket a session lifecycle, and PROCESSING_END is emitted when a processing cycle returns to IDLE.
TOOL_CALL_END carries the full untruncated tool output or full error information even when the ToolResult sent back to the model is truncated.
Tests assert event ordering and payload content through public event consumption APIs.
requirements.notes: Streaming token deltas are part of the event contract even when a specific test uses a non-streaming fake client.

requirements.title: Core agentic processing loop
requirements.summary: Implement process_input as the autonomous turn loop that builds provider-profile requests, calls the low-level LLM client, records assistant turns, executes tool rounds, handles limits, and returns to idle on completion.
acceptance_criteria: process_input appends a UserTurn, emits USER_INPUT, drains pending steering, and enters PROCESSING.

Each model step builds a Request with provider-profile system prompt, converted session history, profile tool definitions, auto tool choice, current reasoning_effort, provider ID, and profile provider_options.
The loop calls Client.complete for non-streaming operation and supports Client.stream for streaming operation while preserving the same tool-round semantics.
Assistant responses append AssistantTurn records containing text, tool calls, reasoning, usage, response ID, and timestamp, then emit assistant text events.
A response with no tool calls is natural completion and exits the loop.
max_tool_rounds_per_input and max_turns stop processing by emitting TURN_LIMIT and leaving the session in IDLE with accumulated history preserved.
Abort signals stop processing, close/cancel active LLM streams and tool processes through the shutdown path, and transition the session to CLOSED.
Queued follow-up messages are processed as a new input after the current input naturally completes.
Multiple sequential submit/process cycles work on one session without losing prior history.
requirements.notes: Use fake LLM clients in deterministic tests; live provider behavior belongs to the smoke-test requirement.

requirements.title: Tool registry and execution pipeline
requirements.summary: Implement provider-profile tool registration, lookup, schema validation, execution, truncation, event emission, and error-result conversion.
acceptance_criteria: ToolDefinition includes a unique name, LLM-facing description, and a JSON Schema parameters object whose root type is object.
RegisteredTool couples a ToolDefinition with an executor callable that receives parsed arguments and an ExecutionEnvironment.
ToolRegistry supports register, unregister, get, definitions, and names, with later registrations replacing earlier tools of the same name.

Tool execution looks up tools by name, validates arguments against the registered schema, executes with the active execution environment, truncates model-facing output, emits TOOL_CALL_START and TOOL_CALL_END, and returns ToolResult values.
Unknown tools, invalid arguments, and executor exceptions are caught and returned to the LLM as is_error=true ToolResult values rather than uncaught exceptions.
Profiles that support parallel tool calls execute multiple calls concurrently, wait for all results, and preserve the original tool-call result order.
Tool execution failures are logged at an appropriate level when diagnostics are useful while preserving recoverable error results for the model.
requirements.notes: Use jsonschema already present in the project for deterministic argument validation where practical.

requirements.title: Shared core tool behavior
requirements.summary: Provide the file, command, search, and glob tool functionality shared by provider profiles, while allowing provider-specific schemas and output conventions.
acceptance_criteria: read_file reads text files through the execution environment, returns line-numbered content in NNN | content format, supports 1-based offset and limit with default limit 2000, reports file-not-found, permission, and binary-file errors as tool errors, and returns image data for multimodal-capable profiles where supported.
write_file writes full file content through the execution environment, creates parent directories when needed, and returns a confirmation including bytes written.

edit_file performs exact old_string/new_string replacement with replace_all defaulting false, reports not found, not unique, file-not-found, and permission outcomes, and may surface fuzzy match information without silently applying ambiguous edits.

shell executes commands through the execution environment, accepts command, timeout_ms, and description arguments, and returns stdout, stderr, exit code, timeout state, and duration in the profile's expected format.

grep searches with regex pattern, optional path, glob_filter, case_insensitive, and max_results default 100, returning file paths and line numbers while reporting invalid regex or missing path as tool errors.
glob returns matching paths under an optional base path sorted by newest modification time first and reports invalid pattern or missing path as tool errors.
Shared tool tests exercise observable filesystem effects and command/search outputs rather than implementation text.
requirements.notes: Provider profiles may expose different schemas while delegating to these shared capabilities.

requirements.title: Execution environment abstraction and local environment
requirements.summary: Implement the ExecutionEnvironment interface and required LocalExecutionEnvironment for filesystem, command, search, lifecycle, and metadata operations.
acceptance_criteria: ExecutionEnvironment exposes read_file, write_file, file_exists, list_directory, exec_command, grep, glob, initialize, cleanup, working_directory, platform, and os_version methods.
ExecResult preserves stdout, stderr, exit_code, timed_out, and duration_ms; DirEntry preserves name, is_dir, and optional size.
LocalExecutionEnvironment resolves file paths relative to its working directory and performs direct filesystem reads, writes, existence checks, and directory listings.

Local command execution uses the platform default shell, starts a new process group where supported, captures stdout and stderr separately, records wall-clock duration, and combines output for tool consumption.

Command timeout defaults to 10000 ms, is bounded by max_command_timeout_ms 600000, and on timeout sends SIGTERM, waits 2 seconds, sends SIGKILL if needed, and returns partial output with the specified timeout message.

Default environment variable filtering excludes case-insensitive *_API_KEY, *_SECRET, *_TOKEN, *_PASSWORD, and *_CREDENTIAL while retaining core variables such as PATH, HOME, USER, SHELL, LANG, TERM, TMPDIR, and language-specific path variables.
Environment inheritance policy supports inherit all, inherit none, and inherit core only behavior.
grep uses ripgrep when available and falls back to a Python regex implementation; glob uses filesystem globbing.
Execution environments can be wrapped for logging or read-only behavior without changing tool implementations.
requirements.notes: Docker, Kubernetes, WASM, and SSH environments are extension examples, not required concrete implementations for this run.

requirements.title: Tool output truncation and context usage warnings
requirements.summary: Apply deterministic model-facing tool output truncation while keeping full output available to hosts, and emit context-window warning events without automatic compaction.
acceptance_criteria: Tool output truncation runs before ToolResult content is sent back to the LLM and never truncates TOOL_CALL_END event output.
Character-based truncation always runs before line-based truncation and handles large single-line outputs.
head_tail truncation keeps the beginning and end of output and inserts a warning that says how many characters were removed from the middle and that full output is available in the event stream.
tail truncation keeps the final max_chars characters and inserts a warning that says how many leading characters were removed and that full output is available in the event stream.

Default character limits are read_file 50000 head_tail, shell 30000 head_tail, grep 20000 tail, glob 20000 tail, edit_file 10000 tail, apply_patch 10000 tail, write_file 1000 tail, and spawn_agent 20000 head_tail.
Default line limits are shell 256, grep 200, glob 500, read_file None, and edit_file None, applied after character truncation using a head/tail omitted-lines marker.
SessionConfig tool_output_limits and tool_line_limits override defaults per tool.
Approximate context usage uses the 1 token per 4 characters heuristic and emits WARNING when history exceeds 80 percent of provider_profile.context_window_size.
Context warning behavior does not automatically compact or summarize conversation history.
requirements.notes: Tests should include pathological long-line output and verify the event stream remains untruncated.

requirements.title: ProviderProfile abstraction and extensibility
requirements.summary: Represent provider-aligned model families with profile objects that supply native tool definitions, prompts, provider options, and capability flags while supporting custom tool overrides.
acceptance_criteria: ProviderProfile exposes id, model, tool_registry, build_system_prompt(environment, project_docs), tools(), provider_options(), supports_reasoning, supports_streaming, supports_parallel_tool_calls, and context_window_size.
OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini profile factories can be created with model identifiers and return profiles with provider-specific tool registries.
Provider profile tools produce ToolDefinition objects suitable for the Unified LLM SDK Request tools field.
Custom tools can be registered on top of a profile after creation.
When a custom tool name collides with a built-in profile tool, the latest registration overrides the previous tool.
Provider profiles preserve provider-native model-facing affordances rather than forcing a single universal edit or shell schema.
requirements.notes: Exact native prompt/tool byte-for-byte fidelity may depend on externally maintained reference agents; tests should verify required observable profile behavior and included tool names.

requirements.title: OpenAI codex-rs-aligned profile and apply_patch
requirements.summary: Implement the OpenAI provider profile with codex-rs-aligned tools and a v4a apply_patch implementation for model-preferred code edits.
acceptance_criteria: The OpenAI profile targets GPT-5+ and codex-family models and exposes read_file, apply_patch, write_file, shell, grep, glob, spawn_agent, send_input, wait, and close_agent tools.
OpenAI file modifications prefer apply_patch; edit_file is not exposed as the primary edit mechanism for this profile.
The OpenAI shell tool uses a 10000 ms default timeout unless explicitly overridden and respects max_command_timeout_ms.
The OpenAI system prompt includes identity, tool usage guidelines, apply_patch format expectations, and coding best practices.
When SessionConfig.reasoning_effort is configured, OpenAI provider options include reasoning.effort in the request path used by the profile.
apply_patch parses patches with Begin Patch and End Patch markers and supports add file, delete file, update file, update plus rename, and multiple hunks in one update block.
apply_patch hunk matching uses context hints plus context lines and reports parse, missing file, and verification errors as tool errors.
When exact hunk matching fails, apply_patch attempts whitespace or Unicode punctuation fuzzy matching before reporting an error.
apply_patch returns affected file paths and operations performed, and tests validate filesystem effects through the public tool interface.
requirements.notes: Implement enough v4a semantics to satisfy observable add/delete/update/rename and hunk matching behavior.

requirements.title: Anthropic Claude Code-aligned profile
requirements.summary: Implement the Anthropic provider profile with Claude Code-aligned tools, old_string/new_string editing, profile-specific shell timeout, prompt guidance, and beta header options.
acceptance_criteria: The Anthropic profile exposes read_file, write_file, edit_file, shell, grep, glob, and subagent tools.
edit_file old_string/new_string is the native editing tool for Anthropic and apply_patch is not exposed for Anthropic models.
The Anthropic read_file tool supports line-numbered output with offset and limit.
The Anthropic shell tool defaults to 120000 ms while still enforcing max_command_timeout_ms and allowing per-call timeout_ms overrides.
The Anthropic grep tool supports ripgrep-backed output modes for content, files_with_matches, and count.
The Anthropic system prompt includes identity, tool selection guidance, read-before-edit guidance, edit_file uniqueness requirements, file operation preferences, and coding best practices.
Anthropic provider options can pass beta headers through provider_options.anthropic.beta_headers.
requirements.notes: Profile behavior must preserve the model-facing old_string/new_string affordance even if shared implementation helpers are reused internally.

requirements.title: Gemini gemini-cli-aligned profile
requirements.summary: Implement the Gemini provider profile with gemini-cli-aligned tools, GEMINI.md project instruction conventions, and Gemini provider options.
acceptance_criteria: The Gemini profile exposes read_file, read_many_files, write_file, edit_file, shell, grep, glob, list_dir, and subagent tools.
web_search and web_fetch are supported as optional Gemini tools when enabled by profile configuration.
Gemini edit_file uses search-and-replace semantics matching gemini-cli conventions while allowing a schema distinct from Anthropic's schema.
The Gemini shell tool defaults to 10000 ms and respects per-call timeout_ms and max_command_timeout_ms.
The Gemini list_dir tool supports directory listing with depth options.
The Gemini system prompt includes identity, tool usage, GEMINI.md conventions, and coding best practices.
Gemini provider options can pass safety settings, grounding configuration, and other Gemini request options through provider_options.gemini.
requirements.notes: web_search and web_fetch are optional profile capabilities; absence must be explicit and testable.

requirements.title: Layered system prompt and project context
requirements.summary: Build provider-specific system prompts from ordered layers of base instructions, environment context, tool descriptions, project documents, and user overrides.
acceptance_criteria: System prompt assembly order is provider-specific base instructions, environment context, tool descriptions, project-specific instructions, and user instruction overrides appended last.
OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini base prompts cover the required provider-specific topics from the spec without relying on a universal prompt.
The environment context block includes working directory, git repository boolean, current git branch, platform, OS version, today's date, model display name, and knowledge cutoff.
Environment context is generated at session start and included in every system prompt built for that session.
Git context captures current branch, short modified/untracked status counts, and the last 5 to 10 commit messages without embedding full diffs.
Project document discovery walks from git root or working directory to the active working directory, loading root-level instruction files first and deeper files later.
AGENTS.md is always loaded; CLAUDE.md is loaded only for Anthropic, GEMINI.md only for Gemini, and .codex/instructions.md only for OpenAI.
Project instruction loading enforces a 32KB total byte budget and appends the specified truncation marker when exceeded.
Tool descriptions in the system prompt reflect the active provider profile's tool definitions.
requirements.notes: Prompt tests should assert required structural behavior and included data, not brittle full prompt text.

requirements.title: Steering, follow-ups, and reasoning effort
requirements.summary: Allow host applications to steer an active or idle session between tool rounds, queue follow-up work, and change reasoning effort for subsequent model calls.
acceptance_criteria: steer(message) queues a message to be injected after the current tool round, or before the next LLM call when the session is idle and later submitted.
drain_steering appends queued steering as SteeringTurn records and emits STEERING_INJECTED events in FIFO order.
SteeringTurn records are converted to user-role messages when building the next LLM request.
follow_up(message) queues a message to be processed as a new input after the current input has reached a text-only model completion.
reasoning_effort accepts low, medium, high, and None, and is passed through to the Unified LLM SDK Request on each model call.
Changing session.config.reasoning_effort during a session affects the next LLM call without mutating prior history.
Provider profiles map reasoning effort into provider options where the profile requires additional provider-native configuration.
requirements.notes: Provider-specific values beyond the core set, such as xhigh, may be accepted only when represented as an explicit extension.

requirements.title: Tool-call loop detection
requirements.summary: Detect repeated tool-call patterns and inject a steering warning so the model can break out of unproductive loops.
acceptance_criteria: Tool-call signatures are derived from tool name plus a stable hash of normalized arguments.
When enable_loop_detection is true, the session checks the last loop_detection_window tool calls after each tool round.
The default window is 10 consecutive tool calls.
detect_loop returns true only when the full window repeats a pattern of length 1, 2, or 3.
On detection, the session appends a SteeringTurn warning instructing the model to try a different approach.
On detection, the session emits LOOP_DETECTION with the warning message.
Loop detection is disabled entirely when enable_loop_detection is false.
requirements.notes: Tests should cover pattern lengths 1, 2, and 3 plus non-repeating near misses.

requirements.title: Subagent sessions and tools
requirements.summary: Implement subagent spawning and coordination tools that run child sessions with independent history on the parent's execution environment.
acceptance_criteria: spawn_agent accepts task, optional working_dir, optional model override, and optional max_turns, and returns an agent ID plus initial status.
send_input accepts agent_id and message and delivers the message to a running subagent.
wait accepts agent_id and returns a SubAgentResult with output text, success boolean, and turns_used.
close_agent accepts agent_id, terminates the child session, and returns final status.
SubAgentStatus represents RUNNING, COMPLETED, and FAILED.
Each subagent owns an independent Session and conversation history.
Subagents share the parent's ExecutionEnvironment and filesystem, optionally scoped to a subdirectory.
Subagents use the parent's ProviderProfile unless a model override is supplied.
Subagent turn limits are independently configurable and default to unlimited.
max_subagent_depth defaults to 1 and prevents recursive sub-subagent spawning.
Subagent results are returned to the parent as tool results, and active subagents are cleaned up during graceful shutdown.
requirements.notes: Implementation may run child sessions as asyncio tasks as long as public semantics and cleanup behavior are deterministic.

requirements.title: Session error handling and graceful shutdown
requirements.summary: Handle tool-level and session-level failures according to the spec, preserving model recoverability for tool errors and deterministic cleanup for aborts or unrecoverable errors.
acceptance_criteria: Tool-level errors including file-not-found, edit conflict, nonzero shell exit, shell timeout, permission denied, validation error, and unknown tool are converted to is_error=true ToolResult values.
LLM transient ProviderError 429, ProviderError 500-503, and NetworkError behavior is delegated to the Unified LLM SDK layer rather than reimplemented in the agent loop.
AuthenticationError surfaces immediately without retry, emits an ERROR event, and transitions the session to CLOSED.
ContextLengthError emits a WARNING event and does not trigger automatic compaction.
TurnLimitExceeded behavior emits TURN_LIMIT and returns the session to IDLE.

Abort or unrecoverable error cleanup cancels any in-flight LLM stream, terminates running command process groups, waits 2 seconds, sends SIGKILL where needed, flushes pending events, closes active subagents, emits SESSION_END with final state, and transitions to CLOSED.
Unexpected exceptions are logged at an appropriate level before being converted to session errors or re-raised according to the public API contract.
requirements.notes: This requirement directly supports the request constraint that exceptions should all be handled or logged at the appropriate level.

requirements.title: Cross-provider parity and smoke validation
requirements.summary: Validate the completed agent against the spec's parity matrix with deterministic tests and optional live smoke tests for each provider profile.
acceptance_criteria: Deterministic tests cover simple file creation, read-then-edit, multi-file edit, shell execution, shell timeout handling, grep plus glob, multi-step read-analyze-edit tasks, large-output truncation, supported parallel tool calls, steering mid-task, reasoning effort changes, subagent spawn and wait, loop detection, tool error recovery, and provider-specific editing format behavior for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini profiles.
Optional live smoke tests are marked or skipped unless required real API keys are present.

Live smoke tests create hello.py, edit it to add a Goodbye print, execute it through the shell tool, verify truncation against a 100000-character file, verify steering changes task scope, verify subagent tool calls appear in events, and verify default timeout handling with sleep 30.
Cross-provider validation asserts observable filesystem effects, events, ToolResults, and session states rather than prompt text or source-code strings.
The deterministic validation gate passes with uv run pytest -q.
The lint gate passes with uv run ruff check .
requirements.notes: Live tests should be opt-in and must not make the default uv run pytest -q depend on network access or API keys.
